question_text: A student investigates four objects at different heights. Which scenario describes the object with the most gravitational potential energy stored?

options.label: A
options.text: A 5 kg object at a height of 8 m

options.label: B
options.text: A 10 kg object at a height of 6 m

options.label: C
options.text: A 8 kg object at a height of 7 m

options.label: D
options.text: A 6 kg object at a height of 9 m

explanation: Gravitational potential energy depends on both mass and height (GPE = mgh). Option B: 10 × 6 = 60 (relative units); Option A: 5 × 8 = 40; Option C: 8 × 7 = 56; Option D: 6 × 9 = 54. Option B has the highest value when mass and height are multiplied together.
